rulings.Case Title: ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. VS. STAN SCHULDINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Of Dfs Services, Llc And Discover Bank
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 22, 2018, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC., THE BANCORP BANK (INCORRECTLY SUED AS THE BANCORP's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Of Dfs Services, Llc And Discover Bank. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Cross-defendants motion for a protective order is continued to May 24, 2018 to be heard by Judge Kahn in Department 302 if necessary. At the April 16 hearing the parties were directed by Judge Kahn to meet and confer on discovery to be taken after service of cross-defendants' motion for summary judgment. If the parties cannot agree to proposed discovery a hearing is scheduled before Judge Kahn on May 24, 2018. The discovery referenced in the within motion is included in Judge Kahn's directive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COMSTOCK HOMES MANAGEMENT 2, LLC, ET AL VS. MBW-BROTHERHOOD WAY LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 22, 2018, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANTS COMSTOCK HOMES MANAGEMENT 2, LLC, ROBERT COMSTOCK'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendants Comstock Homes Management 2, LLC and Robert Comstock's demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract in MBW-Brotherhood Way, LLC's third amended cross-complaint is overruled. A demurrer to a cause of action for breach of contract admits not only the contents of the contract, but also any pleaded meaning that the instrument is reasonably susceptible. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) Paragraph 2 of the Management Services Agreement states that Comstock Homes hired MBW for Hawke's unique talents. Paragraphs 3 and 4 of the MSA provide that the agreement terminates if Hawke ceases to manage the project as contemplated under section 2 for any reason. Paragraph 4 of the MSA also sets forth other grounds for termination (e.g. breach and failure to cure within 10 days). MBW interprets these provisions to mean that the phrase "for any reason" does not include circumstances where Comstock Homes or RCS engages in misconduct and prevents MBW's management responsibilities. MBW alleges that its interpretation prevents the agreement from turning into an at-will agreement and ensures enforcement of the other termination provisions. MBW further alleges that Hawke was managing the project in the manner contemplated by the MSA and Comstock Homes wrongfully terminated the relationship. Lastly, MBW avers that Hawke only stopped managing the project months after September 30, 2014. These allegations cure the deficiencies identified in the order dated February 20, 2018. MBW now states a cause of action for breach of contract. Thrifty Payless, Inc. v. Mariners Mile Gateway, LLC (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1050 is distinguishable because it involved a motion for nonsuit-not a demurrer-and the court noted that the landlord continued to attempt to effectuate the agreement. (Id. at 1054.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for MBW is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER WALKER ET AL VS. HAND JOB, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 22, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT BAO NGUYEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Bao Nguyen's demurrer to all seven causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Jennifer and Andrea Walker is sustained without leave to amend as to all seven causes of action. While the judgment in the prior case in favor of Mr. Nguyen was on an alter ego claim not a fraudulent transfer claim and the facts alleged by the Walkers in this case support fraudulent transfer claims against Mr. Nguyen as "a person for whose benefit the transfer was made" (Qwest Communications Corp. v. Weisz (SD CA 2003) 278 F.Supp.2d 1188), all such claims could have been brought in the prior case and are thus barred by res judicata. "The doctrine [of res judicata] not only precludes re-litigation of claims resolved in a prior action, but it also precludes litigation of claims that could have been brought in the prior action but were not." (Franceschi v. Franchise Tax Board (2016) 1 Cal. App. 5th 247, 258). The Walkers' trial brief in the prior case makes it clear that all facts supporting their claims in this case were known to the Walkers at the time of the trial in the prior case and were sought to be used to support the imposition of alter ego liability against Mr. Nguyen in the prior case. As but one of many references in that trial brief to the same facts alleged in this case, the Walkers' trial brief stated at page 10 of that brief that Mr. Nguyen "transferred Hand Job to his brother in July 2015¿that Hand Job continued to conduct business¿After he transferred the business to his brother, Defendant Nguyen closed the only bank account that Hand Job maintained causing Hand Job to become insolvent." Because the Walkers could have brought all of their fraudulent transfer claims against Mr. Nguyen in the prior case, all those claims are now precluded by res judicata. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Nguyen is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA BERRY ET AL VS. SUTTER VALLEY HOSPITALS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 22, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT SUTTER VALLEY HOSPITALS, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Sutter Valley Hospitals Inc.'s demurrer to the first cause of action for dependent adult abuse/neglect in the complaint is overruled. Plaintiffs' allegations that CPMC violated specific regulations, had a pattern and practice of violating regulations and understaffing to increase profits, and failed to implement the care plan are sufficient to support the claim. (Complaint, pars. 46-53; 59-95; Fenimore v. Regents of the University of California (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 1339.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO ORANGE COUNTY ** PAMELA SALSEDO VS. FCA US, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO TRANSFER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 22, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANTS FCA US, LLC, KATHY FOWLER CHRYSLER-JEEP-DODGE, INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER. Defendants FCA US LLC and Kathy Fowler Chrysler Jeep Dodge Inc.'s motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to Orange County and plaintiff Pamela Salsedo shall pay all required transfer fees. There is no basis for venue in San Francisco. Ms. Salsedo admits that she erroneously believed that FCA did not designate a principal business address in California and could be sued in any county. FCA did designate an Orange County business address. The case is transferred to Orange County rather than Lake County because that is the county that FCA designated per CCP 398(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for FCA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
